question_text: A space probe is launched from Earth (g = 10 N/kg) to Jupiter (g = 25 N/kg). The probe has a mass of 1500 kg. Calculate: (a) The probe's weight on Earth, (b) The probe's weight on Jupiter, (c) The ratio of weights (weight on Jupiter : weight on Earth).
explanation: (a) Weight on Earth = 1500 × 10 = 15,000 N. (b) Weight on Jupiter = 1500 × 25 = 37,500 N. (c) Ratio = 37,500 ÷ 15,000 = 2.5 (or 5:2). This shows Jupiter's much stronger gravity pulls the probe down 2.5 times harder than Earth does.
